concept.title: GUIDES-23211: Text alignment in tables via right-click
concept.shortdesc: Authors need faster access to table text alignment in AEM Guides by exposing alignment actions on the table context menu instead of deep property dialogs.

section.title: Problem statement
section.p: When editing tables in AEM, basic settings such as text alignment and related formatting are buried behind multiple layers of menus. Frequently used actions therefore take longer than necessary during day-to-day authoring.

section.title: Business objective
section.p: Improve table editing in AEM Guides by letting authors set text alignment from a right-click (context) menu on the table or cell selection, rather than only through multi-step property navigation.

ul.li: Type: Customer request; Status: Open; Priority: Major.

Discussion highlights customer frustration with overall table behavior in the Web Editor and asks whether the new editor timeline (on-premise) can move earlier than the currently communicated plan; product response in Jira references new editor planned for Q1 2026.

section.title: DITA align on tables (reference)
section.p: Horizontal alignment for table columns or cells is expressed with the align attribute on colspec, entry, and related table constructs (per DITA 1.x). AEM Guides UI actions for text alignment should map to these values in serialized DITA. Allowed values include:

row.entry: Uses the character specified on the char attribute for alignment.
See DITA spec: use conref target for the effective align value.

section.p: You may set align on colspec (column default) or on individual entry elements to override. Example column defaults: <colspec colname="c1" align="left"/>.
